zh.lifeSummary: Richard Lomas於1513年出生在英國英格蘭藍卡爾。他於1555年11月19日與Agnes Cook在英國藍卡爾法爾沃斯結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和4個女兒的父母。他去世在英國英格蘭爾哈爾頓維德斯法爾沃斯。

nameMeanings.name: Lomas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Manuel, Raul, Angel, Miguel, Guadalupe, Jorge, Pedro, Ana, Armando, Baldo, Esteban, Jesus.
Spanish: habitational name from any of several places called (Las) Lomas, named with the plural of loma ‘hillock, mound’ (see Loma ), or a topographic name for someone who lived by hillocks.

variants.name: Loma
meanings: Spanish: topographic name for someone who lived by a hillock, from loma ‘hillock, mound’ (from Latin lumbus).

variants.name: Lomba
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Manuel, Ana, Carlos, Andres, Beatriz, Eugenio, Fernando, Jorge, Juan, Marcelino, Nydia.
Portuguese and Galician: habitational name from any of several places called Lomba, from lomba ‘mountain ridge’ (compare Lomas ), or a topographic name for someone living by a mountain ridge.

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rikert

He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. en.lifeSummary: Richard Lomas was born in 1513, in Lancashire, England, United Kingdom. He married Agnes Cook on 19 November 1555, in Farnworth, Lancashire, England. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 4 daughters. He died in Farnworth, Widnes, Halton, Cheshire, England, United Kingdom.

ko.Farnworth, Lancashire, England: 영국 잉글랜드 체실르 할턴 위드네스 패른워스
ko.lifeSummary: Richard Lomas(은)는 1513년 영국 잉글랜드 랭카실르에서 태어났다. 그는 1555년 11월 19일 영국 랭카실르 패른워스에서 Agnes Cook(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 영국 잉글랜드 체실르 할턴 위드네스 패른워스에서 사망했다.
